chunks.text: M.S. Advancement to Candidacy - EECS at Berkeley M.S. Advancement to Candidacy Plan I or Plan II? Most EECS M.S. students submit a Plan II thesis report because of its flexibility. You can learn more about the requirements for a Plan I thesis by read

about the requirements for a Plan I thesis by reading the Graduate Division’s Guide . Plan I Plan II Coursework 4-10 units of 299 3-6 units of 299 Advance to Candidacy Department form + University form Department form only Thesis Committee 3 members

m Department form only Thesis Committee 3 members 2 members Thesis Format University format (specific) Department format (flexible) Thesis Availability University Library + EECS Website EECS Website For both Plan I and Plan II (see below) M.S. studen

or both Plan I and Plan II (see below) M.S. students, you need to complete the M.S. Advancement to Candidacy Form . If you are planning to use Plan I for your M.S. Degree, you will need to submit the advancement application through Calcentral . In th

advancement application through Calcentral . In the “Student Resources” Dashboard, under “Submit a Form,” choose “Higher Degrees Committees form.” Candidacy is valid for 3 years. Approximately 95% of EECS graduate students choose Plan 2 for the MS de

EECS graduate students choose Plan 2 for the MS degree. Transfer credit may be awarded for a maximum of 4 semester or 6 quarter units of graduate coursework from another institution. Complete a and submit all supporting documents. Submission Deadline

bmit all supporting documents. Submission Deadline: Both Plan I and Plan II M.S. students need to complete the departmental Advancement to Candidacy form no later than the end of the second week of classes of your final semester. < M.S. Student Guide
